texto: Even the most meticulous historians work subjectively. The historian's point of view, his or her selection of subject and sources, the emphasis, the tone - all of these lead to subjective history, inevitably so. I do not say this as a criticism, merely as an observation.
explicacao: Citação de David Ebershoff sobre temas ligados a trabalho. “Even the most meticulous historians work subjectively. The historian's point of view, his or her …” articula trabalho, work, view em registro de trabalho, com relevância para contextos como trabalho e view.

texto: The agony of martyrdom is almost too much to bear. In the early hours, when the loss is fresh, there is no comfort in knowing Glory will live on. We speak of the martyrs in History but we cannot know the actual pain they suffered in their final living hours. They enter the realm of the mythic, but we must never forget these were men like ourselves. When their flesh is torn, they cry out. They suffer as you or I would suffer, although more bravely. Remember Christ. Although I am now an enemy to Joseph's legacy, I shudder when recalling his pain.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a David Ebershoff: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de limites e escolhas conscientes. O trecho “The agony of martyrdom is almost too much to bear. In the early hours, when the loss is fresh, th…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que vida, martyrs, religion pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de luto e religion.

texto: We are born, we live, we disappear. One of the chilling aspects of history is the swiftness with which it carries us into oblivion.
explicacao: Citação de David Ebershoff sobre sentido da existência e experiência vivida. “We are born, we live, we disappear. One of the chilling aspects of history is the swiftness with …” articula vida, live, born em registro de vida, com relevância para contextos como born e chilling.

texto: Anna's voice wasn't a beautiful voice - rough edged and sorrowful, a bit used, somehow male and female at once. Yet it had more vibrancy to it than most Danish voices, which were often thin and white and too pretty to trigger a shiver. Anna's voice had the heat of the south; it warmed Einar, as if her throat were red with coals.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a David Ebershoff: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de temas ligados a reflexao. O trecho “Anna's voice wasn't a beautiful voice - rough edged and sorrowful, a bit used, somehow male and f…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que reflexao, lgbtq, music pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de termino e music.

texto: My duty, I know, is to lead. I cannot offer an array of options. I must offer a long but narrow path to Salvation, and guide the Saints down it. Were that path wide and varied, it would lead nowhere; and I will then have failed both God and man.
explicacao: Orientação direta de David Ebershoff sobre limites e escolhas conscientes. A afirmação “My duty, I know, is to lead. I cannot offer an array of options. I must offer a long but narrow p…” funciona como lembrete prático ligado a reflexao, religious, offer, útil em momentos de reflexao.
